{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社}} * {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社}} under the modern shrine ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=山口大神宮 |画像=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |所在地=山口県山口市滝町4-4 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 11 | 緯度秒 = 5.2 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 131 |経度分 = 28 | 経度秒 = 5 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP |祭神=天照大御神
豊受大御神 |社格=県社 |創建=[[永正]]17年（[[1520年]]） |本殿=[[神明造]] |別名=高嶺神明、高嶺太神宮、高嶺神社、今伊勢、西のお伊勢さま |地図=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''山口大神宮'''（やまぐちだいじんぐう）は、[[山口県]][[山口市]]にある[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[県社]]。 ==由緒== [[永正]]17年（[[1520年]]）、[[大内氏]]30代目の[[大内義興]]が[[伊勢神宮]]の神霊を[[勧請]]して創建したものである。 永正5年（[[1508年]]）6月、大内義興は山口に逃げてきた前将軍・[[足利義稙]]を奉じて上洛し、以降11年間京都に滞在し、管領代として幕政を執行した。その間の永正11年（[[1514年]]）、義興は伊勢神宮に参拝し、永正15年（[[1518年]]）8月に山口へ戻ると前例のない[[勅許]]を賜った上での[[皇太神宮]]、[[豊受大神宮]]の[[大内義興#伊勢神宮の勧請|山口への勧請]]をしたいと思ったという。義興は、山口に帰国してすぐに伊勢神宮を勧請するための準備を始めた。伊勢神宮を勧請するための土地を探して、現在地の高嶺東麓を社地に定め、すぐに社殿建立を開始した。永正16年（[[1519年]]）[[11月3日]]に外宮、永正17年（[[1520年]]）[[4月8日]]に内宮を造営すると、[[吉田神社]]を通じて天皇に伊勢神宮勧請の許可を求め、[[後柏原天皇]]の勅許を得、て同年[[6月29日]]神宮の御師 高向二頭大夫光定により伊勢神宮の神霊を[[勧請]]、鎮座した『高嶺太神宮御鎮座伝記』『山口県史 資料編 中世2』。当初は'''高嶺神明'''と称していたが、後柏原天皇から「高嶺太神宮」、[[後陽成天皇]]から「伊勢」の[[宸筆|御宸筆]]勅額が授けられ、'''高嶺太神宮'''、'''今伊勢'''と呼ばれるようになった。[[式年遷宮]]は[[伊勢神宮遷宮前後相論]]に配慮し1540年に外宮・内宮同時に行われている。 当時の伊勢神宮は幕府の神宮頭人の力が及ばなくなり、全国の荘園から届く[[役夫工米]]が滞納された事により慢性的な財政難に陥り、外宮は[[1434年]]内宮は[[1463年]]より[[式年遷宮]]が中断していた。その反面、庶民による[[伊勢信仰]]は広まり南北朝時代頃よりには飛び神明といわれる伊勢神宮の勧請が流行し各地に[[神明神社]]が創建されていた。高嶺大神宮の勧請もそうした流れを受けたものであったが、江戸時代まで、伊勢神宮より勅許を得て神霊の勧請を受けたのは日本中で当社だけであった{{efn|江戸期に入ると伊勢商人の手によって[[相模国]]に勧請され[[伊勢原大神宮]]が創建され式年遷宮もとり行われた。『[[新編相模国風土記稿]]』によると伊勢原村に「當村開墾の頃勧請せし所にて、伊勢の神廟に擬し、二十一年目毎に社頭修理を加へ、遷宮の式あり」と記載されている{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}。}}。江戸時代に[[伊勢信仰]]が盛んになると、山口大神宮には特に西日本各地から多くの参拝者が訪れるようになった。今も参道脇には多くの「日参一万日成就」の石碑が残る。 昭和3年、県社に列せられ、社名を'''高嶺神社'''とした。昭和22年に現在の山口大神宮に改称した。 ==境内== 伊勢神宮と同様の内宮・外宮があり、それぞれに附属した別宮・摂社がある。これらの社殿は隣接して建っており、石段を降りた所に祈祷などが行われる神楽殿がある。 *内宮 -- 主祭神 [[天照大神|天照大御神]]、配祀 [[アメノタヂカラオ|天手力男命]]・[[栲幡千千姫命|萬幡豊秋津姫命]] **内宮別宮 荒祭宮 -- 祭神 天照大御神[[荒魂]]・[[祓戸大神|瀬織津姫命]] **内宮摂社 恵美須社 -- 祭神 [[事代主|事代主命]] *外宮 -- 主祭神 [[トヨウケビメ|豊受大御神]]、配祀 [[ニニギ|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命]]・[[天児屋命|天児屋根命]]・[[フトダマ|天太玉命]]・[[建御名方神]]・八坂刀自命 **内宮別宮 多賀宮 -- 祭神 豊受大御神荒魂・[[祓戸大神|伊吹戸主神]] 境内には摂社 多賀神社、末社 高嶺稲荷神社があり、[[鴻ノ峰]]中腹に岩戸社がある。鴻ノ峰登山口は外宮の前にあり、岩戸社までは徒歩約10分である。 *多賀神社 -- 主祭神 [[イザナギ|伊弉諾尊]]・[[イザナミ|伊弉冉尊]]、配祀 天照大御神・[[スサノオ|素戔嗚命]]・[[スクナビコナ|少名彦名神]]・[[カグツチ|火具土神]]・[[国之常立神|国常立命]]・[[応神天皇]]・[[菅原道真]]命・[[大内教弘]]命 *高嶺稲荷神社 *岩戸社 -- 祭神 天照大御神・天手力男命 ==祭事== 当初は、伊勢神宮と同様の20年に一度の[[式年遷宮]]が行われていたが、戦乱などにより延期されたり、簡単な改修のみで済ませたりということも行われるようになった。明治以降は明治12年、明治35年に行われたが、その後一時中断した。昭和35年に社殿建替が行われ、[[平成]]12年（[[2000年]]）に式年遷宮が行われた。内宮・外宮の敷地の横には式年遷宮の際の古殿地がある。 ==文化財== {{節スタブ}} ==交通アクセス== *[[山口駅 (山口県)|山口駅]]からバスで約5分、県庁前バス停下車。徒歩約5分。 *山口駅から徒歩約25分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{notelist}} === 出典 === {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == 外部リンク == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ 山口大神宮 - 西のお伊勢さま] == 関連項目 == * [[神明神社]] * [[大神宮]] {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:やまくちたいしんくう}} [[Category:山口県の神社]] [[Category:山口市の建築物]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]] [[Category:山口県の旧県社]] [[Category:神明神社]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]]

{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi City|qq=}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi Yoshitaka|qq=}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi clan|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshitane|qq=}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|qq=}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|qq=}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]]

The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi City|qq=}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi Yoshitaka|qq=}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi clan|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshitane|qq=}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|qq=}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|qq=}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]]

Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Yamaguchi Grand Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]]

{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=[[File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG|260px]] |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}
{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall={{ill|Shimmei-zukuri|ja|神明造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shimmei-zukuri|qq=}} |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Yamaguchi City|ja|山口市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi City|qq=}}, Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshitaka|ja|大内義興|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi Yoshitaka|qq=}}, the 30th head of the {{ill|Ōuchi clan|ja|大内氏|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōuchi clan|qq=}}. The shrine enshrines the deities of {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|伊勢神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|qq=}}, specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun {{ill|Ashikaga Yoshitane|ja|足利義稙|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashikaga Yoshitane|qq=}} and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from {{ill|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|ja|後柏原天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|qq=}} to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|qq=}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Ame-no-Koyane|ja|天児屋命|1=Ame-no-Koyane|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Koyane|qq=}}, {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from {{ill|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|ja|山口駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)|qq=}} to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * {{ill|Shinmei Shrine|ja|神明神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinmei Shrine|qq=}} * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]]

{{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi }} '''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=}} under the modern shrine Ranking system. == History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi. Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''. In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|qq=}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed. During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach. The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947. == Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=}}. * Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]]

Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=}}, {{ill|Takeminakata|ja|建御名方神|1=Takeminakata|2=ja|3=タケミナカタ|lt=Takeminakata|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeminakata|qq=}}, {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=}}. Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno. == Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000. == Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station. == References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} == External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū] == See also == * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}} {{Munakata Faith}} {{translated page|ja|山口大神宮|version=86365238|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 3]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:History of Yamaguchi City]] [[Category:Ken-sha]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Munakata Faith]] }} [[sn:Yamaguchi Daijingu]] [[ja:山口大神宮]] [[sn:amaguchi Daijingu]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Yamaguchi (city)]] [[Category:山口市の歴史]]
