{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11466743]] Wikidata name: Yamaguchi Daijingū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi|qid=Q207876}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.184777777777775|N|131.46805555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |founded_by = {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshioki|qid=Q909270}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}

It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.

Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.

However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.

Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山口大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11466743]] Wikidata name: Yamaguchi Daijingū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|qid=Q2983724}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi|qid=Q207876}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.184777777777775|N|131.46805555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |founded_by = {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshioki|qid=Q909270}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545}} }}
'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|qid=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū]
== See also == * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}}

'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.

During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.

However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. * Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.

Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|qq=|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.

The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山口大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11466743]] Wikidata name: Yamaguchi Daijingū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi|WD=Q207876}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.184777777777775|N|131.46805555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |founded_by = {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshioki|WD=Q909270}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山口大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11466743]] Wikidata name: Yamaguchi Daijingū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi|WD=Q207876}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.184777777777775|N|131.46805555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |founded_by = {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshioki|WD=Q909270}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū]
== See also == * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山口大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11466743]] Wikidata name: Yamaguchi Daijingū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi|WD=Q207876}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.184777777777775|N|131.46805555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |founded_by = {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshioki|WD=Q909270}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū]
== See also == * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}}

It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.

On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.

'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.

* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.

The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.

* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:山口大神宮]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11466743]] Wikidata name: Yamaguchi Daijingū [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name=Yamaguchi Daijingū | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Yamaguchidaijingu.JPG |location=Takimachi 4-4, Yamaguchi City, Yamaguchi Prefecture |LatDeg = 34 |LatMin = 11 |LatSec = 5.2 | N = N |LonDeg = 131 |LonMin = 28 |LonSec = 5 | E = E |MapCountryCode = JP |deity={{ill|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602}}; {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|WD=Q2983724}} |ShrineRank=Prefectural Shrine |consecration_year=[[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) |MainHall=[[:en:Shinmei-zukuri|Shimmei-zukuri]] |Alias=Takanemine Shinmei, Takanemine Daijingū, Takanemine Jinja, Imaise, Nishi no O-Ise-sama |Map=Japan Yamaguchi |location = {{ill|Yamaguchi|WD=Q207876}} |coordinates = {{Coord|34.184777777777775|N|131.46805555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |founded_by = {{ill|Ōuchi Yoshioki|WD=Q909270}} |architecture_style = {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|WD=Q930545}} }}
'''Yamaguchi Daijingū''' (山口大神宮) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Yamaguchi (city)|Yamaguchi City]], Yamaguchi Prefecture. It was formerly designated as a {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|lt=Prefectural shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the modern shrine Ranking system.
== History == Yamaguchi Daijingū was founded in [[:en:Eishō|Eishō]] 17 (1520) by [[:en:Ōuchi Yoshioki|Ōuchi Yoshitaka]], the 30th head of the [[:en:Ōuchi clan|Ōuchi clan]]. The shrine enshrines the deities of [[:en:Ise Shrine|Ise Grand Shrine]], specifically {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}} and {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}.
In Eishō 5 (1508), Ōuchi Yoshitaka supported the former shogun [[:en:Ashikaga Yoshitane|Ashikaga Yoshitane]] and accompanied him to Kyoto. During his 11-year stay in the capital, Yoshitaka visited Ise Grand Shrine in Eishō 11 (1514). After returning to Yamaguchi in Eishō 15 (1518), he sought and received imperial permission from [[:en:Emperor Go-Kashiwabara|Emperor Go-Kashiwabara]] to enshrine the deities of Ise in Yamaguchi.
Construction of the shrine began at the eastern base of Mount Takamine, with the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Outer Shrine|ja|外宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Outer Shrine|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=豊受大神宮}} completed in Eishō 16 (1519) and the {{ill|Kōtai Jingū|lt=Kōtai Jingū|lt=Inner Shrine|ja|内宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inner Shrine|qq=}} in Eishō 17 (1520). On June 29 of the same year, the deities were ceremonially transferred from Ise by priest Takamukai Nito Daibu Mitsusada. Initially called '''Takamine Shinmei''', the shrine later received the imperial titles '''Takamine Daijingū''' and '''Imaise'''.
In its early years, the shrine conducted {{ill|Sengū|lt=Shikinen-sengu|ja|式年遷宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sengū|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=遷宮}}, or periodic rebuilding, similar to Ise Grand Shrine. However, as conflicts and financial constraints arose, this practice was modified or postponed.
During the Edo period, the spread of {{ill|Ise faith|ja|伊勢信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise faith|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=伊勢神宮}} drew numerous pilgrims from western Japan to the shrine. It was known as "Nishi no O-Ise-sama" (Western Ise Shrine). Many stone monuments commemorating pilgrimages still stand along the approach.
The shrine was designated a Prefectural Shrine in 1928, renamed '''Takamine Shrine''', and finally adopted its current name, Yamaguchi Daijingū, in 1947.
== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}

== Grounds == The shrine complex includes both an Inner Shrine and an Outer Shrine, along with associated subsidiary and auxiliary shrines. The buildings replicate the layout and architectural style of Ise Grand Shrine.
* Inner Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Amaterasu-Ōmikami|ja|天照大御神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu-Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=天照大神}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja|天手力男命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Tajikarao|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=アメノタヂカラオ|1=Ame-no-Tajikarao|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q849412}}, {{ill|Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja|萬幡豊秋津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamanoya-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=栲幡千千姫命|1=Takuhadachiji-hime no Mikoto|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11110848}}. ** Inner Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri-no-Miya|qq=}} – Deities: {{ill|Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|天照大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Kami|lt=Haraedo no Ōkami|ja|瀬織津姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Haraedo no Ōkami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=瀬織津姫}}. ** Inner Shrine Subordinate Shrine: Ebisu Shrine – deity: {{ill|Kotoamatsukami|ja|事代主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoamatsukami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=事代主}}.
* Outer Shrine – Main deity: {{ill|Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja|豊受大御神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-Ōmikami|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=トヨウケビメ|1=Toyōke Ōmikami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2983724}}. Additional deities: {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja|天津彦彦火瓊々芸命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, [[:en:Ame-no-Koyane|Ame-no-Koyane]], {{ill|Futodama|ja|天太玉命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futodama|ja_comment=fixed jawiki redirect|3=フトダマ}}, [[:en:Takeminakata|Takeminakata]], {{ill|Yasakatome|ja|八坂刀自命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yasakatome|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. ** Outer Shrine Auxiliary Shrine: Taga Shrine – Deities: {{ill|Toyouke-hime|lt=Toyouke-Ōmikami (Aramitama)|ja|豊受大御神荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyouke-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Iwanaga-hime|ja|伊吹戸主神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwanaga-hime|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
Additionally, the precincts feature Taga Shrine and Takamine Inari Shrine, along with Iwato Shrine, located on the mountainside of Mount Kōno.
== Annual Events == Initially, Yamaguchi Daijingū performed Shikinen-sengu every 20 years, mirroring Ise Grand Shrine. Although interrupted by wars and financial constraints, the practice resumed intermittently in the modern era. Major reconstructions occurred in 1880 and 1902. The most recent Shikinen-sengu was held in 2000.
== Access == * 5 minutes by bus from [[:en:Yamaguchi Station|Yamaguchi Station (Yamaguchi)]] to Kenchō-mae Bus Stop, then a 5-minute walk. * Approximately 25 minutes on foot from Yamaguchi Station.
== References == * {{Cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之46村里部大住郡巻之5|chapter=糟屋庄 伊勢原村 神明社|id={{NDLJP|1179219/19}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 伊勢原村 神明社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}}
== External Links == * [http://www.yamaguchi-daijingu.or.jp/ Official site of Yamaguchi Daijingū]
== See also == * [[:en:Shinmei shrines|Shinmei Shrine]] * {{ill|Daijingū|ja|大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Daijingū|qq=}}
